content.text:
```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Spot Price Spike Triggers Utility Volatility Crush",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) daily",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When natural gas spot prices jump >8% in a single day, utilities face immediate margin compression and hedging repricing. This creates a short-term tactical opportunity as the market reprices utility equity risk.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Utilities are direct consumers of natural gas input costs; sudden price spikes compress earnings guidance and trigger sell-offs in dividend stocks."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "XLU closes down >1.5% on a day when DHHNGSP rises >8% from prior close",
      "exit": "XLU closes up >1% from entry or 7 calendar days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Natural gas prices are volatile; 8%+ daily moves occur 3–5 times per quarter, making this a realistic monthly signal."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Lumber Futures Volatility Spike Signals Construction Cost Shock",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance continuous lumber futures (CL=F) daily price via price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Lumber prices swing sharply on housing demand shocks and forestry disruptions. A 5%+ daily move in lumber signals incoming margin pressure for homebuilders and building materials suppliers.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrials and building-materials companies face near-term input cost inflation; the market reprices earnings downward."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "CL=F rises or falls >5% in a single day; simultaneously XLI closes down >0.8%",
      "exit": "XLI outperforms SPY by >1% over 2 consecutive days or 10 calendar days pass",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Lumber futures exhibit >5% swings 2–4 times monthly due to weather, mills, and inventory news."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Open-Metoe Heavy Rain Forecast Triggers Agricultural Commodity Bounce",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo weather API 14-day precipitation forecast for US Corn Belt (centroid: Iowa)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When forecasts shift to heavy rain (>1.5 inches predicted within 7 days) in the Corn Belt, crop stress eases and commodity prices rebound on favorable moisture outlook.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Agricultural commodity prices rise on improved crop conditions; materials and mining stocks often co-move with commodity sentiment."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Open-Meteo forecast updates to >1.5 inches rain within 7 days AND agricultural commodity ETFs (DBC, AGF) rise >1.2% same day",
      "exit": "Agricultural ETF closes down >0.5% from entry or 8 calendar days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weather forecasts update daily; rain forecast swings occur 2–3 times per month during growing season."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Google Trends Spike In 'Recession Proof Job' Searches Signals Labor Market Anxiety",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'recession proof jobs' and 'job security'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When job-security searches surge (>40% week-over-week), labor market sentiment deteriorates. Workers fear layoffs, signaling incoming unemployment data will disappoint.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary companies depend on wage growth and job security; rising job anxiety predicts lower discretionary spending."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Google Trends 'recession proof jobs' index jumps >40% week-over-week; XLY closes down >0.6% same week",
      "exit": "XLY closes up >0.8% from entry or 3 weeks elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Job anxiety spikes emerge 1–2 times per month around earnings seasons and macro shocks."
  },
  {
    "idea": "RSS Feed Count Surge On Freight Congestion News Triggers Bearish Logistics Rotation",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed count aggregation for 'freight congestion', 'trucking backlog', 'port delay' across major logistics news feeds",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When logistics/freight RSS mentions spike (>25% daily jump), transportation bottlenecks are intensifying. This predicts higher costs and demand headwinds.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrial transport and logistics companies face margin pressure; supply-chain stress triggers sector rotation out of cyclicals."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "RSS count for freight/congestion keywords jumps >25% day-over-day; IYT (transportation ETF) closes down >1% same day",
      "exit": "IYT closes up >1.2% from entry or 5 trading days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Freight news volatility generates >25% RSS spikes 1–2 times per week on average."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Count Growth Slowdown Signals EV Subsidy Cliff Ahead",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap daily EV charger count (US total) via openchargemap adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly EV charger growth drops below 0.3% (i.e., rollover in new installations), it signals waning subsidy tailwinds and slowing EV infrastructure buildout sentiment.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "EV-related tech and semiconductor stocks rally on infrastructure optimism; slowdown in charger deployment signals reduced future demand for EV chips."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Weekly EV charger growth (7-day percent change) falls below 0.3% AND semiconductor ETF (SOXX) closes down >0.9% on same week",
      "exit": "SOXX closes up >1% from entry or 2 weeks elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "EV infrastructure growth is lumpy; 0.3% weekly threshold is crossed 2–3 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Activity Spike In Refining Regions Signals Energy Supply Risk Premium",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity API for magnitude >4.0 events in US Gulf Coast / California refining zones",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When earthquake activity picks up in major refining regions (Gulf Coast, California), energy prices spike on supply-disruption fear. Crude and refined-product margins widen.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Energy stocks benefit from higher crude prices and refinery margin expansion when supply disruption fears emerge."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "USGS logs 1+ magnitude >4.0 earthquake in Gulf Coast / California refining zone; crude oil (CL=F) closes up >2% same day; XLE closes up >1.5%",
      "exit": "XLE closes down >0.8% from entry or 6 trading days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Refining-zone earthquakes >4.0 occur 1–2 times per month on average; crude is highly sensitive to supply shock fears."
  },
  {
    "idea": "VIX Intraday Crush Into Close Signals Options Gamma Squeeze Bounce",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance intraday VIX price (^VIX) via price_only adapter, 15-minute bars",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When VIX drops >15% from intraday high to close (gamma squeeze), options dealers are forced to buy equity hedges into close, triggering a momentum bounce the next day.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Gamma-driven rallies lift risk appetite; consumer discretionary and growth sectors lead the bounce."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "VIX intraday high to close decline >15%; SPY closes flat or down; buy SPY at close",
      "exit": "SPY closes up >0.8% from entry or 3 trading days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "VIX intraday volatility and gamma squeezes occur 2–4 times per week on average."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Steel Price Surge On China Manufacturing PMI Surprise Triggers Industrials Rally",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series CSCICP03CHM (China Manufacturing PMI) weekly + Yahoo Finance steel futures (USO proxy) daily",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When China PMI beats expectations by >1 point AND steel prices surge >3%, global demand expectations improve. Industrials and materials rally on improved cycle outlook.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrial companies depend on global construction and manufacturing demand; China PMI strength signals near-term capex and infrastructure spend."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "China PMI data beats consensus by >1 point; steel/materials ETF (DBB or SLV) rises >2% same week; XLI closes up >0.5%",
      "exit": "XLI closes down >0.6% from entry or 10 calendar days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "China PMI is released monthly; beats/misses occur ~40% of the time, making this a realistic signal 1–2 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "High Yield Bond Spread Widening Spike Signals Credit Fear Flight-to-Quality",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series BAMLH0A0HYM2 (High Yield OAS spread) daily",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When HY OAS spreads widen >30 bps in a single day, credit stress emerges. Flight-to-quality rotations out of consumer discretionary and cyclicals into utilities and staples.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Credit tightening signals economic stress and weakening consumer balance sheets; discretionary spending declines."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "HY OAS widens >30 bps day-over-day; XLY closes down >0.7%; simultaneous buy XLP (staples)",
      "exit": "HY OAS tightens >15 bps from widening day or 6 trading days elapse",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Credit spreads fluctuate sharply 1–2 times per week; >30 bps daily moves occur 2–3 times per month on average."
  }
]
```
